name: Crystal Structure & Unit Cells
description: Determining a crystal structure starts from the lattice-and-basis description: a unit cell and its Bravais lattice are identified, close-packed arrangements are classified as FCC/HCP versus BCC, coordination number and atomic packing factor are computed, and Miller indices label planes for relating structure to properties.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Crystalline solid"]
    N2["Identify lattice + basis"]
    N3["Choose unit cell"]
    N4["Assign Bravais lattice"]
    N5{"Close-packed?"}
    N6["FCC / HCP"]
    N7["BCC or other"]
    N8["Compute coordination + APF"]
    N9["Miller indices for planes"]
    N10["Structure-property model"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 -->|yes| N6
    N5 -->|no| N7
    N6 --> N8
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Introduction to Solid State Physics (8th ed.)
Materials Science and Engineering: An Introduction (10th ed.)
sources.authors: Callister, W. D.; Rethwisch, D. G.
